skill.name: retraction-watcher
skill.description: Automatically scan document reference lists and check for retracted, corrected, or questionable papers in academic reference lists.
skill.summary: Scans academic reference lists and cross-checks citations against retraction databases to identify retracted papers.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 69 static findings. All 14 high-risk findings (weak crypto, path traversal) are false positives: 'MIT' license strings were flagged as weak crypto, and a security checklist item about preventing path traversal was flagged as traversal itself. All 34 medium-risk external_commands findings are markdown code block formatting misidentified as shell backtick execution. 19 low-risk network findings include 6 false positives (documentation URLs) and 13 true positives (legitimate HTTP calls to Crossref, PubMed, and Open Retractions APIs). The 2 filesystem findings include 1 false positive (security checklist) and 1 true positive (user-specified report output). The skill is a transparent academic integrity tool with no obfuscation, credential theft, or data exfiltration patterns. Network and filesystem access are essential to its core function.

low_findings.title: HTTP Requests to Academic APIs
low_findings.description: The script makes HTTP requests to Crossref API, PubMed E-utilities, and Open Retractions for citation integrity checking. These are legitimate academic database queries essential to the skill's function.

low_findings.title: File Write for Report Output
low_findings.description: The script writes report output to a user-specified file path via the --output argument. Path is controlled by the user, not hardcoded.

content.user_title: Check References Against Retraction Watch Database
content.value_statement: Researchers often cite retracted papers without knowing, which damages their work's credibility. This skill scans reference lists and cross-checks citations against retraction databases to identify problematic references before publication.
actual_capabilities: Parses citations from PDF, BibTeX, RIS, and plain text files
Checks DOIs against Crossref API for retraction metadata
Checks PMIDs against PubMed for retraction notices
Generates detailed, summary, and JSON reports with retraction status
Extracts references section from full documents automatically
Caches API results to avoid duplicate requests during batch checks
limitations: Requires internet connection for all database lookups
Rate limits may apply to free API tiers from Crossref and PubMed
Very recent retractions under 48 hours may not be indexed yet
Title-only matching may produce false positives with similarly titled papers

use_cases.title: Pre-submission Manuscript Review
use_cases.description: Check manuscript references before journal submission to ensure no retracted papers are cited in the bibliography.

use_cases.title: Literature Review Validation
use_cases.description: Verify the integrity of all references in a literature review or thesis bibliography before final submission.

use_cases.title: Journal Editorial Integrity Checks
use_cases.description: Scan submitted manuscripts for citations to retracted or questionable papers as part of the editorial review process.

prompt_templates.title: Quick PDF Reference Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan this manuscript PDF for any references to retracted papers: [upload file]
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic check of a single PDF document

prompt_templates.title: BibTeX Bibliography Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this BibTeX bibliography file for retracted publications and provide a detailed report with status for each reference.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Reference Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse these references and cross-check against all available retraction databases including Crossref, PubMed, and Open Retractions.

prompt_templates.title: Full Research Integrity Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this document for reference integrity. Extract all citations, check against retraction databases, and provide a structured report with status categories and recommendations.

output_examples.input: Check references.bib for retracted papers before my submission
output_examples.output: Report showing retracted, expression of concern, corrected, and clear citations with specific recommendations for each flagged reference

output_examples.input: Scan manuscript.pdf for citation issues in the bibliography
output_examples.output: Summary report with retracted papers highlighted in red, including retraction dates, reasons, and urgent action items for removal

output_examples.input: Verify these DOIs against retraction databases
output_examples.output: JSON report with per-citation status codes, matched database records, and check timestamps for all verified identifiers

best_practices: Provide DOIs when available for the most accurate retraction matching against databases
Review each flagged citation manually before making final decisions about removal
Run checks close to submission deadlines for the most current database data
anti_patterns: Using title-only matching as the sole verification method without manual review of results
Ignoring expressions of concern which may indicate future retractions are pending
Checking references only once at the start of long research projects

faq.question: What databases does this skill check against?
faq.answer: It checks Crossref API, PubMed E-utilities, and Open Retractions database for retraction records.

faq.question: Does it work without internet access?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires an active internet connection for all database lookups.

faq.question: Can it check PDF files directly?
faq.answer: Yes, it can extract text from PDF files using PyPDF2 to find and check references.

faq.question: How accurate is the retraction detection?
faq.answer: DOI-based checks are highly accurate. Title-based matching uses a 90% similarity threshold.

faq.question: Does it detect very recent retractions?
faq.answer: Retractions less than 48 hours old may not appear in the databases yet.

faq.question: What file formats are supported?
faq.answer: PDF, BibTeX, RIS, plain text files, and direct text input are all supported.
